references.title: Using ditavalref in a map results in no content (PDF/HTML output empty)
references.snippet:
Concept primer: <ditavalref> is a DITA element placed inside a map to apply a DITAVAL filter to a branch of the map inline, rather than passing a filter via args.filter on the command line. It enables per-branch filtering within a single publication.

Issue #4713 (open) — label: bug
Title: Using ditavalref in a map results in no content
URL: https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/issues/4713

Affected versions: DITA-OT 4.3.3 and later (regression from 4.3.2)
Environment: Windows 11, OpenJDK 19.0.1, custom PDF plugin

Expected behavior:
A map containing <topicref> elements and a <ditavalref> child should produce a PDF (or HTML5) with full topic content, filtered according to the referenced DITAVAL. In DITA-OT 4.3.2 and earlier, the merged XML (_MERGED.xml) contained the complete map structure with all topics included.

Actual behavior (DITA-OT 4.3.3+):
All map content disappears from the output. The merged XML contains only the outer map structure — no topic content. Front and back matter pages may still render, but body content is absent. Root cause traced to a change in dost.jar introduced in 4.3.3: copying that JAR from 4.3.3 back into a 4.3.2 installation reproduces the problem, confirming the regression.

Workaround:
Pass the DITAVAL file via the args.filter parameter instead of using <ditavalref>. This restores full content but loses per-branch filtering capability.

Reproduction steps:
1. Create a DITA map with several <topicref> elements.
2. Add a <ditavalref href="filter.ditaval"/> inside the map.
3. Run: dita -i map.ditamap -f pdf2 (or -f html5).
4. Inspect output and _MERGED.xml — body content is missing.

Attachments: none listed in issue body.

Disclaimer: Open community-reported GitHub issue. Verify against your installed DITA-OT version. Not a formal spec ruling.

references.title: keydef target file copied to output even when filtered out by DITAVAL
references.snippet:
Concept primer: A <keydef> can point to a non-DITA resource (PDF, TXT, ZIP) as its href target. When a DITAVAL filter excludes the keydef's scope, those resource files should not be copied to the output folder — they are irrelevant to the filtered publication.

Issue #4729 (open) — label: bug
Title: keydef target file is copied to output folder even when filtered out by ditaval
URL: https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/issues/4729

Affected version: DITA-OT 4.3.5
Environment: Linux, html5 transformation

Expected behavior:
Only keydef target files (PDFs, TXTs, etc.) that survive DITAVAL filtering should be copied to the output folder. If a keydef is excluded by the filter, its target resource must not appear in the output.

Actual behavior:
All keydef target files are copied to the output folder regardless of DITAVAL filtering. Reporter's real-world impact: a map with 7 product-version keydefs, each pointing to a ~35 MB PDF, resulted in all 7 PDFs (≈245 MB) copied to every filtered output even when only one product version was included.

Root cause (reporter hypothesis):
In preprocess2, the step that collects and copies resource files runs before DITAVAL filtering is applied — so filtering decisions are not yet available when the copy list is assembled.

Reproduction steps:
1. Create a map with multiple keydefs pointing to large binary files, each scoped to a different product value.
2. Create a DITAVAL that excludes all but one product value.
3. Run: dita -i map.ditamap --filter filter.ditaval -f html5
4. Inspect output folder — all keydef target files are present, not just the included product's file.

Attachments: sample.zip (referenced in issue; contains reproduction map, keydefs, DITAVAL, and binary stubs).

Disclaimer: Open community-reported GitHub issue. Verify against your installed DITA-OT version. Not a formal spec ruling.

references.title: Keyrefs are not resolved in DITA 2.x documents
references.snippet:
Concept primer: A keyref is a DITA mechanism to indirectly reference a defined value (key). A <keydef> in the map assigns a name (e.g. "product") to a value (e.g. "MyProduct"). Elements like <ph keyref="product"/> should be replaced by that value in output. DITA 2.x introduced schema changes; this issue reports keyref resolution breaking under that doctype.

Issue #4758 (open) — label: bug
Title: Keyrefs are not resolved in DITA 2.x
URL: https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/issues/4758

Affected version: DITA-OT 4.4
Environment: Windows, dita CLI and oXygen XML Editor
Transformation types affected: HTML5 and PDF2

Expected behavior:
A <ph keyref="product"/> in a DITA 2.x topic should resolve to the value defined in the map's keydef (e.g. "MyProduct") and appear as that text in the rendered output.

Actual behavior:
The keyref element remains empty or unresolved in both HTML5 and PDF2 output. The key definition exists and is syntactically correct, but DITA-OT 4.4 does not expand it when the source files use DITA 2.x doctypes/namespaces.

Example:
  Map: <keydef keys="product"><topicmeta><keywords><keyword>MyProduct</keyword></keywords></topicmeta></keydef>
  Topic: <ph keyref="product"/> — renders as empty string instead of "MyProduct".

Reproduction steps:
1. Create a DITA 2.x map declaring the DITA 2.x namespace/doctype.
2. Add a keydef mapping "product" to "MyProduct" via topicmeta/keywords.
3. Reference it in a topic: <ph keyref="product"/>.
4. Run: dita -i map.ditamap -f html5 (or -f pdf2).
5. Observe empty output where "MyProduct" should appear.

Attachments: none listed in issue body.

Disclaimer: Open community-reported GitHub issue. Verify against your installed DITA-OT version. Not a formal spec ruling.

references.title: Hierarchical filtering: no WARN emitted when child include/flag conflicts with excluded parent
references.snippet:
Concept primer: In DITA conditional processing with subject schemes, a DITAVAL rule for a parent value (e.g. exclude linux) takes precedence over a conflicting child rule (e.g. include redhat) — because redhat IS-A linux. The DITA 1.3 spec (§5.2.4) calls this "an error condition" and says applications MAY recover by generating an error message. DITA-OT currently processes this silently.

Issue #4766 (open) — labels: ai-generated, bug, needs reproduction
Title: Hierarchical filtering: emit WARN diagnostic when child include/flag conflicts with excluded parent
URL: https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/issues/4766

Affected version: DITA-OT 4.4

Expected behavior:
When a DITAVAL file sets a parent value to exclude but sets a child value to include or flag, DITA-OT should emit a WARN-level diagnostic (similar in style to existing message DOTJ049W) identifying the conflict. This helps authors discover content modeling mistakes before content silently disappears.

Actual behavior:
DITA-OT processes the conflict silently — the parent exclusion wins (child content is excluded), but no warning is emitted. The behavior is also inconsistent across filter modules (BranchFilterModule vs. the main filter pipeline).

Affected cases (from hierarchical-filtering-cases.zip):
- Case 1: linux=exclude, redhat=include → redhat content silently excluded, no WARN
- Case 1b: linux=exclude, redhat=flag → redhat content silently excluded, no WARN

Spec reference: DITA 1.3 §5.2.4 — conflict between parent exclude and child include/flag is "an error condition."

Reproduction steps:
1. Create a subject scheme with linux → redhat hierarchy on @platform.
2. Create case1.ditaval: default exclude @platform; linux=exclude; redhat=include.
3. Run: dita -i test.ditamap --filter case1.ditaval -f html5
4. Observe: no WARN in build log, redhat content missing.

Attachments: hierarchical-filtering-cases.zip (test map, subject scheme, all 16 DITAVAL case files, README).

Disclaimer: Open community-reported GitHub issue. Verify against your installed DITA-OT version. Not a formal spec ruling.

references.title: Hierarchical filtering: child explicit include drops inherited parent flag action
references.snippet:
Concept primer: In DITA subject scheme hierarchies, attribute values can have parent/child relationships (e.g. @platform: linux is parent of redhat). A DITAVAL can set different actions on parent and child values. When the parent is flagged and the child is explicitly included, the question is whether the parent's flag styling is inherited alongside the child's include — additive behavior.

Issue #4768 (open) — labels: ai-generated, bug, needs reproduction
Title: Hierarchical filtering: child explicit include drops inherited parent flag action
URL: https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/issues/4768

Affected version: DITA-OT 4.4

Expected behavior (per DITA 1.3 spec):
When platform="linux" is set to flag and platform="redhat" is set to include in the DITAVAL, content marked platform="redhat" should be included AND carry the inherited linux flag (change bar, color highlight, or other flag styling). Since redhat IS-A linux, the parent's flag is additive.

Actual behavior:
Content marked platform="redhat" is included in the output but the linux flag action is silently dropped. The child's explicit include resolves the action and DITA-OT stops processing without applying the parent's flag.

Root cause hypothesis:
DITA-OT's BranchFilterModule resolves the child's explicit action first; finding include, it considers resolution complete without consulting the parent's flag rule.

Affected case:
- Case 4: linux=flag, redhat=include → redhat content included but unflagged

Reproduction steps:
1. Create subject scheme: @platform with linux (parent) → redhat (child).
2. DITAVAL case4.ditaval: platform="linux" action="flag" (with flagging style); platform="redhat" action="include".
3. Topic: paragraphs marked platform="linux" and platform="redhat".
4. Run: dita -i test.ditamap --filter case4.ditaval -f html5
5. Inspect output: redhat paragraph appears but lacks linux flag styling.

Attachments: hierarchical-filtering-cases.zip (subject scheme, all case DITAVAL files, test map, README).
Related issue: #3856 (BranchFilterModule hierarchy handling), #4766, #4769.

Disclaimer: Open community-reported GitHub issue. Verify against your installed DITA-OT version. Not a formal spec ruling.

references.title: Hierarchical filtering: child include/flag does not propagate upward to unspecified parent
references.snippet:
Concept primer: In DITA subject scheme hierarchies, attribute values form parent/child trees (e.g. @platform: linux is parent of redhat). A DITAVAL default="exclude" excludes all values not explicitly listed. The question is whether explicitly including/flagging a child value (redhat) should also include content marked with its unspecified parent (linux) — called upward propagation.

Issue #4769 (open) — labels: ai-generated, bug, needs reproduction
Title: Hierarchical filtering: child include/flag does not propagate upward to unspecified parent
URL: https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/issues/4769

Affected version: DITA-OT 4.4

Expected behavior (per DITA 1.3 spec §5.2.4):
If DITAVAL sets @platform default=exclude and explicitly includes or flags only the child value redhat, content marked platform="linux" (the unspecified parent) should also be included. Rationale: all RedHat content is Linux content; upward propagation from child to unspecified parent should apply.

Actual behavior:
Content marked platform="linux" remains excluded even when redhat is explicitly included or flagged. DITA-OT only implements downward propagation (parent action propagates to children) — upward propagation from child to unspecified parent is missing.

Affected cases:
- Case 6: @platform default=exclude; redhat=include; linux=unspecified → linux content incorrectly excluded
- Case 6b: @platform default=exclude; redhat=flag; linux=unspecified → linux content incorrectly excluded

Root cause:
DITA-OT's filtering pipeline implements parent→child propagation only. The symmetrical child→unspecified-parent propagation required by the spec is not implemented.

Reproduction steps:
1. Create subject scheme: @platform enumerationdef with linux (parent) → redhat (child).
2. DITAVAL case6.ditaval: att element default=exclude; platform redhat action=include (linux not listed).
3. Topic: paragraphs marked platform="linux" and platform="redhat".
4. Run: dita -i test.ditamap --filter case6.ditaval -f html5
5. Inspect output: linux paragraph is absent; only redhat paragraph appears.

Attachments: hierarchical-filtering-cases.zip (16 filtering cases, subject scheme, test map, expected vs actual README).
Related issues: #4768 (flag inheritance), #4766 (missing WARN diagnostic).

Disclaimer: Open community-reported GitHub issue. Verify against your installed DITA-OT version. Not a formal spec ruling.
